summary: Security hardening testing completed. All 24 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend auth flow verified: login stores both tokens, logout clears localStorage. All new security features working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: A1-A6: Refresh token flow fully implemented - login returns both tokens, refresh rotates tokens, logout revokes tokens, old tokens rejected
A7-A9: Frontend AuthContext correctly stores/clears tokens in localStorage, has 401 interceptor for auto-refresh
B1: OAuth state persistence implemented in DB via OAuthState model with 10 min TTL - replaces in-memory dict
C1-C6: WAF middleware blocks SQL injection (DROP, UNION SELECT, SLEEP/BENCHMARK) and XSS (script tags, javascript:, event handlers)
JWT access token correctly set to 15 minutes expiry (verified via JWT decode)
Refresh token rotation working - old tokens are deleted after use
All regression tests pass - admin login, public programs, calendar availability, debug endpoints require auth, ICS feeds require tokens
context_for_next_testing_agent: Security hardening testing completed. All features verified: (A) Refresh tokens with rotation and revocation, (B) OAuth state persistence in DB, (C) WAF middleware blocking SQL/XSS. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_security_hardening.py with 24 tests. Previous security audit tests in test_security_audit.py still valid.

verified_features.feature: A1: POST /api/auth/login returns both 'token' and 'refresh_token' fields
A2: Access token expires in ~15 minutes (JWT 'exp' field verified)
A3: POST /api/auth/refresh with valid refresh_token returns new token pair
A4: POST /api/auth/refresh with old (rotated) refresh_token returns 401
A5: POST /api/auth/logout revokes the refresh token
A6: POST /api/auth/refresh after logout returns 401
A7: Frontend login stores both 'token' and 'refresh_token' in localStorage
A8: Frontend auto-refreshes token when 401 received (axios interceptor in AuthContext.js lines 74-113)
A9: Frontend logout calls /api/auth/logout and clears localStorage
B1: Microsoft Calendar /connect endpoint persists OAuth state in DB (OAuthState model)
B1: /connect requires auth, /status returns connection status
C1: WAF blocks SQL injection DROP TABLE pattern
C2: WAF blocks UNION SELECT pattern
C3: WAF blocks XSS <script> tags in query params
C4: WAF blocks XSS in POST body
C5: WAF allows normal legitimate requests through
C6: WAF blocks SLEEP/BENCHMARK injection
WAF blocks javascript: protocol XSS
WAF blocks event handler XSS (onerror, onclick)
Regression: Admin login and dashboard still works
Regression: Public programs endpoint still works
Regression: Calendar availability still works
Regression: Debug endpoints require auth
Regression: ICS feeds require tokens
